search_terms_used: running economy determinants systematic review oxygen cost running biomechanics training pubmed
Saunders 2004 running economy factors affecting distance running performance review pubmed
strength training running economy improvement meta-analysis resistance plyometric endurance runners pubmed
Barnes Kilding 2015 running economy review strategies improvement distance runners Sports Medicine pubmed
Conley Krahenbuhl 1980 running economy distance runners variability performance predictor pubmed
Fletcher Esau MacIntosh 2009 running economy review changes fitness training oxygen cost pubmed
coverage_notes: Strong coverage: foundational performance prediction (Conley & Krahenbuhl 1980), comprehensive determinants review (Saunders 2004), measurement validity (Shaw 2014, Fletcher 2009), measurement norms (Barnes & Kilding 2015a), improvement strategies (Barnes & Kilding 2015b), biomechanical factors (Moore 2016, Van Hooren 2024 meta-analysis), and strength training effects (Balsalobre-Fernández 2016, Llanos-Lagos 2024 meta-analyses). Includes 2 meta-analyses and running-specific evidence throughout.

papers.title: Running economy and distance running performance of highly trained athletes
papers.notes: Demonstrated that running economy explained 65.4% of 10km performance variation among 12 elite runners with similar VO2max values.

papers.title: Factors affecting running economy in trained distance runners
papers.notes: Comprehensive review of physiological and biomechanical factors affecting running economy in trained distance runners.

papers.title: Economy of running: beyond the measurement of oxygen uptake
papers.notes: Compared running economy expressed as oxygen cost versus energy cost in 16 trained male distance runners across three submaximal speeds.

papers.title: The valid measurement of running economy in runners
papers.notes: Evaluated the validity of oxygen cost as a running economy measure by comparison with underlying energy cost in runners.

papers.title: Running economy: measurement, norms, and determining factors
papers.notes: Reviewed how running economy is defined, measured, and the physiological and biomechanical factors that determine it.

papers.title: Strategies to improve running economy
papers.notes: Reviewed acute and chronic interventions to improve running economy including endurance training, HIIT, resistance training, plyometrics, altitude, and nutrition.

papers.title: Effects of Strength Training on Running Economy in Highly Trained Runners: A Systematic Review With Meta-Analysis of Controlled Trials
papers.journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
papers.notes: Meta-analysis of controlled trials showing strength training programs improve running economy in highly trained runners.

papers.title: Is There an Economical Running Technique? A Review of Modifiable Biomechanical Factors Affecting Running Economy
papers.notes: Reviewed modifiable biomechanical factors affecting running economy, finding propulsion phase biomechanics play a particularly important role.

papers.title: Effect of Strength Training Programs in Middle- and Long-Distance Runners' Economy at Different Running Speeds: A Systematic Review with Meta-analysis
papers.notes: Meta-analysis examining how different strength training methods affect running economy at various speeds, finding high load and combined methods most effective.

papers.title: The Relationship Between Running Biomechanics and Running Economy: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Observational Studies
papers.notes: Systematic review and meta-analysis finding running biomechanics explain 4-12% of between-individual variation in running economy, with vertical displacement and stiffness being most significant.
